description: No-code agentic AI platform for security risk and governance professionals

tasks.title: Project Foundation & Dependencies
tasks.description: Initialize Next.js project with all required dependencies
steps: Create agentforge-app/ using: npx create-next-app@latest agentforge-app --typescript --tailwind --app --src-dir --import-alias '@/*'
Install auth: npm install next-auth@beta @auth/supabase-adapter
Install Supabase: npm install @supabase/supabase-js @supabase/ssr
Install React Flow: npm install @xyflow/react
Install utilities: npm install clsx tailwind-merge lucide-react class-variance-authority
Install LangGraph + LangChain.js: npm install @langchain/langgraph @langchain/core @langchain/openai @langchain/anthropic @langchain/google-genai @langchain/community
Install form: npm install react-hook-form zod @hookform/resolvers

Initialize shadcn/ui: npx shadcn@latest init â€” choose Dark theme, CSS variables: yes. Then install core components: npx shadcn@latest add button card input label textarea select dialog dropdown-menu tabs toast tooltip badge separator skeleton avatar

Confirm .mcp.json exists at agentforge-app/.mcp.json with shadcn, magic, and playwright MCP servers (file already created â€” just verify it is present and fill in placeholder API keys from .env.local)
Create .env.local from the environment variables listed in architecture.md (all values set to placeholder strings â€” real keys filled in by human operator)

Apply the dark color palette from ui_ux_design.md to agentforge-app/src/app/globals.css â€” replace the default shadcn CSS variables with the security-professional dark navy palette defined in the design doc
Create src/lib/utils.ts with cn() helper using clsx + tailwind-merge

Create src/types/index.ts with shared TypeScript types matching the data model in architecture.md: User, Organization, Solution, WorkflowNode, WorkflowEdge, KnowledgeBase, KBDocument, DataConnector, SolutionRun, AuditLog

tasks.title: Supabase Database Schema
tasks.description: Create all database tables, indexes, and enable pgvector
steps: Create supabase/migrations/001_enable_pgvector.sql: enable pgvector extension
Create supabase/migrations/002_organizations.sql: organizations table (id, name, domain, sso_provider, sso_config jsonb, created_at)
Create supabase/migrations/003_users.sql: users table (id, org_id FK, email, display_name, avatar_url, role, sso_subject, last_login_at, created_at). Role enum: admin | builder | viewer

Create supabase/migrations/004_solutions.sql: solutions table (id, creator_id FK, org_id FK, title, description, category, visibility, status, architecture, metadata jsonb, run_count, avg_rating, published_at, created_at, updated_at)
Create supabase/migrations/005_workflow.sql: workflow_nodes table and workflow_edges table as defined in architecture.md
Create supabase/migrations/006_knowledge_bases.sql: knowledge_bases, kb_documents, document_chunks (with vector(1536) column), data_connectors tables

Create supabase/migrations/007_runs.sql: solution_runs table (id, solution_id FK, user_id FK, status, input_data jsonb, output_data jsonb, node_traces jsonb, total_tokens, duration_ms, error_message, started_at, completed_at). Also create langgraph_checkpoints table (thread_id text PK, checkpoint jsonb, metadata jsonb, created_at, updated_at) â€” used by the custom SupabaseSaver checkpointer to persist and resume LangGraph graph state
Create supabase/migrations/008_audit.sql: audit_logs table (id, user_id, org_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, metadata jsonb, ip_address, created_at)
Create supabase/migrations/009_indexes.sql: add indexes on org_id, creator_id, kb_id, solution_id foreign keys. Add ivfflat index on document_chunks.embedding for vector search
Create supabase/migrations/010_rls.sql: Row Level Security policies â€” users can only see resources matching their org_id or visibility='public'. Admins can see all within their org
Create supabase/seed.sql: insert a demo organization and admin user for local dev
Document each migration with inline comments

tasks.title: Supabase Client Setup
tasks.description: Create Supabase client helpers for server and browser
steps: Create src/lib/supabase/server.ts: createServerClient() using @supabase/ssr for Server Components and API Routes
Create src/lib/supabase/client.ts: createBrowserClient() singleton for Client Components
Create src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts: updateSession() middleware helper
Create src/middleware.ts: apply Supabase session refresh middleware, protect all routes except /login and /api/auth/*
Create src/lib/db/users.ts: getUserById(), createUser(), updateUser(), upsertUserFromSSO()

tasks.title: Enterprise SSO Authentication â€” Azure AD
tasks.description: Implement NextAuth.js with Azure Active Directory OIDC
steps: Create src/auth.ts: configure NextAuth with MicrosoftEntraID provider (Azure AD). Map email, name, image from token claims
Create src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts: export GET and POST handlers
In callbacks.signIn: upsert user record in Supabase users table using sso_subject from profile.sub
In callbacks.session: attach user.id, user.role, user.org_id from DB to the session object
Create src/app/login/page.tsx: login page with 'Sign in with Azure AD' button and optional Ping Identity button. Show organization branding area
Create src/components/auth/SignInButton.tsx: button component that calls signIn(provider)
Test: after Azure AD sign-in callback returns, user record should exist in users table

tasks.title: Authentication Middleware & RBAC
tasks.description: Protect routes and enforce role-based access control
steps: Update src/middleware.ts: if no session, redirect to /login for any non-public route
Create src/lib/auth/rbac.ts: defineRoles() mapping (admin: all, builder: create/edit solutions + KB, viewer: read + run only). Export hasPermission(role, action) function
Create src/lib/auth/session.ts: getServerSession() wrapper that returns typed session with user.role
Create src/components/auth/AuthGuard.tsx: client component that checks session and role, shows 403 if insufficient permission
Create src/app/unauthorized/page.tsx: 403 unauthorized page
Apply role checks to API routes: admin-only routes return 403 for non-admin users

tasks.title: App Shell & Navigation Layout
tasks.description: Create the main application shell with sidebar navigation
steps: Create src/app/(app)/layout.tsx: authenticated app layout wrapper that checks session, renders sidebar + main content area

Create src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx: left sidebar with navigation links (Dashboard, My Solutions, Knowledge Base, Marketplace, Admin). Highlight active route. Collapse to icon-only on small screens
Create src/components/layout/Header.tsx: top header with page title, user avatar dropdown (profile, sign out), and notification bell
Create src/components/layout/UserMenu.tsx: dropdown with user display name, email, role badge, sign out button
Create src/components/ui/Badge.tsx: role and status badge component with color variants
Create src/components/ui/Button.tsx: button component with variants (primary, secondary, ghost, destructive)
Create src/components/ui/Card.tsx: card wrapper component
Create src/components/ui/Input.tsx: styled input component
Create src/components/ui/Textarea.tsx: styled textarea component
Create src/components/ui/Select.tsx: styled select component using Radix UI
Create src/components/ui/Dialog.tsx: modal dialog component using Radix UI
Create src/components/ui/Toast.tsx: toast notification component using Radix UI
Create src/components/ui/Spinner.tsx: loading spinner
Create src/components/ui/Skeleton.tsx: skeleton loading placeholder

tasks.title: Dashboard Home Page
tasks.description: Build the main dashboard after login
steps: Create src/app/(app)/page.tsx: dashboard page (server component)
Show welcome message with user's display name
Show stats cards: My Solutions count, Knowledge Bases count, Total Runs, Marketplace solutions available
Show 'Quick Actions' section: 'Create New Solution' button, 'Upload to Knowledge Base' button, 'Browse Marketplace' button
Show 'Recent Activity' section: last 5 solution runs with status, duration, and link to run detail
Show 'Getting Started' section for new users with 3-step guide (only visible if user has 0 solutions)
Create src/lib/db/dashboard.ts: getDashboardStats(userId, orgId) query function
Test in browser: verify all sections render with mock data

tasks.title: Solutions CRUD â€” API & Data Layer
tasks.description: Backend API routes and DB layer for solutions management
steps: Create src/lib/db/solutions.ts: createSolution(), getSolutions(userId, orgId), getSolutionById(id), updateSolution(id, data), deleteSolution(id), publishSolution(id), duplicateSolution(id)
Create src/app/api/solutions/route.ts: GET (list with filter by status/category) and POST (create)
Create src/app/api/solutions/[id]/route.ts: GET, PATCH, DELETE â€” verify ownership before mutation
Create src/app/api/solutions/[id]/publish/route.ts: POST â€” set status=published, visibility from body, set published_at
Create src/app/api/solutions/[id]/duplicate/route.ts: POST â€” deep copy solution with all nodes and edges
Create src/lib/db/workflow.ts: saveWorkflow(solutionId, nodes, edges), getWorkflow(solutionId)
Create src/app/api/solutions/[id]/workflow/route.ts: GET and PUT for workflow canvas state
All mutations write to audit_logs table

tasks.title: Solutions List Page
tasks.description: UI for listing and managing user's solutions
steps: Create src/app/(app)/solutions/page.tsx: solutions list page (server component)
Fetch solutions with status filter tabs: All / Draft / Published / Archived
Display solution cards showing: title, description, category badge, visibility badge, status badge, architecture type, run count, last updated date
Each card has action menu: Edit / Run / Duplicate / Archive / Delete
Create src/components/solution/SolutionCard.tsx: card component
Create src/components/solution/SolutionStatusBadge.tsx: colored status indicator
Add 'Create New Solution' button at top right (builder role and above)
Empty state with call-to-action when no solutions exist
Test in browser: verify list renders, filter tabs work, empty state shows

tasks.title: New Solution Setup Page
tasks.description: Form to configure a new solution before opening the builder
steps: Create src/app/(app)/solutions/new/page.tsx: multi-step setup form
Step 1 â€” Basic Info: title (required), description, category dropdown (risk_assessment, compliance, threat_intel, incident_response, audit, other), visibility selector

Step 2 â€” Architecture: show 5 architecture template cards with diagram previews and descriptions (Single Agent, Sequential Chain, Parallel Fan-out, Hierarchical, Router, Blank Canvas). User selects one
Submit: call POST /api/solutions, then redirect to /solutions/:id/edit
Create src/components/solution/ArchitectureCard.tsx: clickable card with icon, name, description, and topology diagram preview
Create src/components/solution/NewSolutionForm.tsx: multi-step form with progress indicator
Test in browser: complete both steps and verify solution is created and redirects to builder

tasks.title: Agent Workflow Builder Canvas
tasks.description: Core drag-and-drop canvas for composing agent workflows using React Flow
steps: Create src/app/(app)/solutions/[id]/edit/page.tsx: builder page (client component)
Create src/components/builder/WorkflowCanvas.tsx: React Flow canvas with custom node types and edge types
Register custom node types: AgentNode, InputNode, OutputNode, RouterNode, AggregatorNode
Create src/components/builder/nodes/AgentNode.tsx: agent node card showing label, LLM provider icon, model name, system prompt preview. Has configure button
Create src/components/builder/nodes/InputNode.tsx: start node (green border, label 'User Input')
Create src/components/builder/nodes/OutputNode.tsx: end node (blue border, label 'Final Output')
Create src/components/builder/nodes/RouterNode.tsx: router/decision node with branching handles
Create src/components/builder/nodes/AggregatorNode.tsx: merge node that combines parallel inputs

Pre-populate canvas with nodes based on selected architecture template (from solution.architecture field). Templates map to LangGraph patterns: single=one AgentNode; sequential=Inputâ†’AgentNodeâ†’AgentNodeâ†’Output; parallel=Inputâ†’[AgentNode,AgentNode]â†’AggregatorNodeâ†’Output; hierarchical=Inputâ†’AgentNode(supervisor)â†’[AgentNode,AgentNode]â†’AgentNode(supervisor)â†’Output; router=Inputâ†’RouterNodeâ†’AgentNode(selected)â†’Output
Add React Flow controls: MiniMap, Controls panel, Background grid
Create src/components/builder/Toolbar.tsx: left toolbar with draggable node palette (drag to add new nodes)
Implement auto-save: debounce 2s after any canvas change, call PUT /api/solutions/:id/workflow
Show save status indicator: 'Saved' / 'Saving...' / 'Unsaved changes'
Test in browser: verify canvas loads with template nodes, can drag new nodes, can connect nodes with edges

tasks.title: Node Configuration Panel
tasks.description: Slide-out panel to configure each agent node's LLM and prompt settings
steps: Create src/components/builder/NodeConfigPanel.tsx: right-side slide panel that opens on node click

Panel sections: Node Label (editable), LLM Provider (dropdown: OpenAI / Anthropic / Azure OpenAI / Gemini), Model (dropdown populated based on selected provider), System Prompt (large textarea with variable hints), LLM Parameters (temperature slider 0-2, max tokens input), Knowledge Bases (multi-select of user's available KBs), Human-in-the-Loop toggle (when enabled, LangGraph will interruptBefore this node â€” a human must approve before it executes), Advanced (JSON schema for structured output optional)
Create src/components/builder/LLMProviderSelect.tsx: provider + model selector component with provider icons
Create src/components/builder/SystemPromptEditor.tsx: textarea with character count, variable hints ({user_input}, {kb_context}, {previous_output})
Create src/components/builder/KBAttachSelect.tsx: searchable multi-select for knowledge bases
On save: update the node data in React Flow state, triggering auto-save
Test in browser: click a node, configure LLM + prompt, verify data persists on refresh

tasks.title: Knowledge Base â€” API & Data Layer
tasks.description: Backend for knowledge base CRUD and document management
steps: Create src/lib/db/knowledge.ts: createKB(), getKBs(userId, orgId), getKBById(id), updateKB(id, data), deleteKB(id), addDocument(kbId, docData), deleteDocument(docId), getDocuments(kbId)
Create src/app/api/knowledge/route.ts: GET (list) and POST (create KB)
Create src/app/api/knowledge/[id]/route.ts: GET, PATCH, DELETE

Create src/app/api/knowledge/[id]/documents/route.ts: POST (upload file â€” receive multipart/form-data, store in Supabase Storage under knowledge/:kbId/:filename, create kb_documents record with status=pending, trigger processing job)
Create src/app/api/knowledge/[id]/documents/[docId]/route.ts: DELETE (remove from storage and DB, cascade delete chunks)
Create src/app/api/knowledge/[id]/search/route.ts: POST { query, topK } â€” generate query embedding via OpenAI, run pgvector similarity search, return top chunks
Create src/lib/storage/documents.ts: uploadToStorage(), deleteFromStorage(), getSignedUrl() helpers

tasks.title: Knowledge Base â€” Document Processing Pipeline
tasks.description: Process uploaded documents using LangChain.js loaders, splitters, and SupabaseVectorStore
steps: Create src/lib/processing/loader.ts: loadDocument(storagePath, fileType) â€” download file from Supabase Storage to a temp buffer, then use LangChain loaders: PDFLoader for PDF, DocxLoader for DOCX, TextLoader for TXT/CSV. Returns array of LangChain Document objects with pageContent and metadata

Create src/lib/processing/splitter.ts: splitDocuments(docs) â€” use LangChain RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter({ chunkSize: 512, chunkOverlap: 50 }) to split Document objects into chunks. Preserve source metadata (filename, page number)

Create src/lib/processing/vectorstore.ts: upsertChunks(kbId, chunks, supabase) â€” use SupabaseVectorStore.fromDocuments(chunks, new OpenAIEmbeddings({ model: 'text-embedding-3-small' }), { client: supabase, tableName: 'document_chunks', queryName: 'match_chunks' }). Also create embedQuery(text) for single query embedding at retrieval time

Create supabase/migrations/012_match_chunks_fn.sql: PostgreSQL function match_chunks(query_embedding vector, match_threshold float, match_count int, filter_kb_id uuid) used by SupabaseVectorStore for similarity search

Create src/lib/processing/pipeline.ts: processDocument(documentId, supabase) â€” orchestrates: load â†’ split â†’ upsertChunks â†’ update kb_documents status='ready' and chunk_count. On any error: update status='error' with error message

Create src/app/api/knowledge/[id]/documents/process/route.ts: POST { documentId } â€” trigger pipeline (called internally after upload completes). Update kb.document_count and kb.chunk_count aggregates

Create src/app/api/knowledge/[id]/search/route.ts: POST { query, topK=5 } â€” use SupabaseVectorStore.similaritySearch(query, topK) filtered to this KB's chunks, return results with content and relevance score

tasks.title: Knowledge Base â€” Data Connectors
tasks.description: Support SharePoint, Confluence, and web URL connectors
steps: Create src/lib/connectors/types.ts: ConnectorConfig interface, ConnectorSyncResult interface
Create src/lib/connectors/web-crawl.ts: crawlUrls(urls[]) â€” fetch each URL, strip HTML (use a simple regex or DOMParser), return array of {url, text, title}

Create src/lib/connectors/confluence.ts: fetchConfluencePages(config) â€” call Confluence REST API with Basic Auth or token, return page content. Stub implementation with placeholder for real credentials
Create src/lib/connectors/sharepoint.ts: fetchSharePointFiles(config) â€” call Microsoft Graph API with OAuth token. Stub implementation for now
Create src/lib/connectors/sync.ts: syncConnector(connectorId) â€” fetch content from connector, process each page as a document through the pipeline, update last_synced_at
Create src/app/api/knowledge/[id]/connectors/route.ts: POST (add connector), GET (list connectors)
Create src/app/api/knowledge/[id]/sync/route.ts: POST â€” trigger connector sync job

tasks.title: Knowledge Base â€” UI Pages
tasks.description: Frontend pages for managing knowledge bases
steps: Create src/app/(app)/knowledge/page.tsx: KB list page showing all KBs as cards (name, description, doc count, status, visibility)
Create src/components/knowledge/KBCard.tsx: card with status indicator (empty/processing/ready/error), document count, last updated
Create src/app/(app)/knowledge/new/page.tsx: create KB form (name, description, visibility)
Create src/app/(app)/knowledge/[id]/page.tsx: KB detail page
Create src/components/knowledge/DocumentList.tsx: list of uploaded documents with status, size, delete button
Create src/components/knowledge/DocumentUpload.tsx: drag-and-drop file upload area supporting PDF, DOCX, TXT, CSV (max 50MB). Shows upload progress and processing status
Create src/components/knowledge/ConnectorPanel.tsx: section to add data connectors (type selector, config form, sync button, last synced time)
Create src/components/knowledge/KBSearch.tsx: search bar to test semantic search against the KB, shows results with relevance score
Test in browser: upload a PDF, verify it appears in list with processing status, then ready status

tasks.title: LangGraph Agent Execution Runtime
tasks.description: Compile and execute LangGraph StateGraphs from stored workflow definitions, streaming results via SSE
steps: Create src/lib/runtime/checkpointer.ts: SupabaseSaver class implementing LangGraph's BaseCheckpointSaver interface. Methods: put(config, checkpoint, metadata) and get(config) â€” read/write to langgraph_checkpoints table in Supabase

Create src/lib/runtime/llm-factory.ts: getLangChainModel(provider, model, apiKey, config) â€” returns the correct LangChain chat model: ChatOpenAI / ChatAnthropic / AzureChatOpenAI / ChatGoogleGenerativeAI based on provider string. Passes temperature and maxTokens from node config

Create src/lib/runtime/kb-retriever.ts: getKBRetriever(kbIds[], supabase) â€” creates a SupabaseVectorStore retriever for each attached KB, returns a combined retriever. Uses OpenAIEmbeddings for query embedding

Create src/lib/runtime/graph-compiler.ts: compileWorkflow(solutionId, supabase) â€” loads workflow_nodes and workflow_edges from DB, constructs LangGraph StateGraph with AgentState (userInput, messages, nodeOutputs), adds nodes via addNode(), adds edges via addEdge() and addConditionalEdges(), sets entry/finish points, compiles with SupabaseSaver checkpointer. Returns compiled graph + list of HITL node IDs (nodes with hitl_enabled=true in node_config)

Create src/lib/runtime/node-builder.ts: buildAgentNode(node, supabase) â€” returns a LangGraph node function that: (1) retrieves KB context if kbIds are set, (2) builds system prompt by substituting {kb_context} and {previous_output} variables, (3) calls the LangChain model, (4) returns state update with new nodeOutputs entry

Create src/lib/runtime/router-builder.ts: buildRouterNode(node) â€” returns a LangGraph conditional edge function that calls LLM with a classification prompt and returns the next node ID as a routing decision

Create src/app/api/solutions/[id]/run/route.ts: POST handler â€” (1) resolve org LLM keys (decrypt from DB, fallback to env), (2) create solution_runs record with status=running, (3) compile graph via graph-compiler, (4) call compiled.streamEvents({userInput}, {configurable: {thread_id: runId}}) with interruptBefore set to HITL node IDs, (5) pipe events to SSE response. SSE event types: {type:'node_start', nodeId}, {type:'token', nodeId, content}, {type:'node_complete', nodeId, output}, {type:'interrupt', nodeId, state} (for HITL), {type:'done', runId}, {type:'error', message}

Create src/app/api/runs/[runId]/resume/route.ts: POST { approved: boolean, feedback?: string } â€” load checkpoint via SupabaseSaver, call graph.updateState() to inject human decision, resume graph.streamEvents() from checkpoint, pipe SSE to response
Create src/app/api/runs/[runId]/route.ts: GET â€” return solution_runs record with current status and output
On graph completion: update solution_runs (status=completed, output_data, node_traces from streamEvents metadata, total_tokens, duration_ms). Increment solution.run_count
Resolve LLM API keys priority: (1) org-level encrypted key from llm_provider_keys table, (2) fallback to OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env vars

tasks.title: Solution Run Page
tasks.description: UI to run a solution and see streaming output
steps: Create src/app/(app)/solutions/[id]/run/page.tsx: solution run page
Show solution title and description at top
Create src/components/run/InputForm.tsx: dynamic form for user to enter the initial input text (textarea)

Create src/components/run/ExecutionPanel.tsx: displays streaming execution output. Shows node-by-node progress as nodes execute. Each node shows: node label, LLM provider badge, streaming token output in a pre-formatted box. Handle all SSE event types: node_start (show node as active), token (append to node output), node_complete (mark node done), interrupt (show HITL panel), done (show final output), error (show error state)

Create src/components/run/HITLApprovalPanel.tsx: shown when an SSE 'interrupt' event is received. Displays the pending node label, its pending input/state, and two buttons: 'Approve & Continue' (POST /api/runs/:runId/resume { approved: true }) and 'Reject' (POST /api/runs/:runId/resume { approved: false }). Reopens SSE stream after resume to continue receiving output
Use EventSource API to consume the SSE stream from /api/solutions/:id/run
Show execution flow visualization: highlight which node is currently running in a simplified graph view
Show final aggregated output prominently after execution completes
Show run stats footer: total tokens used, duration, run ID
Create src/components/run/RunHistoryList.tsx: list of past runs with status, date, and link to run detail
Create src/app/(app)/solutions/[id]/history/page.tsx: run history page
Test in browser: run a solution, verify streaming output appears node by node

tasks.title: Solution Marketplace
tasks.description: Browse and discover published solutions from the organization
steps: Create src/app/(app)/marketplace/page.tsx: marketplace browse page
Fetch solutions where visibility='public' OR (visibility='org' AND org_id matches current user's org)
Display as grid of cards with: title, description, category, architecture type, creator name, run count, rating stars, published date
Create src/components/marketplace/MarketplaceCard.tsx: marketplace solution card
Filter sidebar: by category, by architecture type, search by keyword
Create src/app/(app)/marketplace/[id]/page.tsx: solution detail page
Show full description, creator info, category, architecture diagram (simplified), run count, ratings
'Run This Solution' button (links to run page), 'Copy to My Solutions' button (calls /api/solutions/:id/duplicate)
Show reviews/ratings section
Create src/app/api/marketplace/route.ts: GET with filter params
Create src/app/api/marketplace/[id]/rating/route.ts: POST to submit rating (1-5 stars)

tasks.title: Admin Panel â€” User & Org Management
tasks.description: Admin pages for managing users and organization settings
steps: Create src/app/(app)/admin/layout.tsx: admin layout that gates on role='admin', redirects non-admins to /unauthorized
Create src/app/(app)/admin/users/page.tsx: user management table. Show email, display name, role, last login, created date
Each user row has role change dropdown (admin can change other users' roles)
Create src/app/api/admin/users/route.ts: GET (list org users)
Create src/app/api/admin/users/[id]/route.ts: PATCH { role } to update user role
Create src/app/(app)/admin/org/page.tsx: org settings page. Show org name, domain, SSO provider config display
Create src/app/api/admin/org/route.ts: GET and PATCH org settings
Create src/lib/db/admin.ts: getOrgUsers(orgId), updateUserRole(userId, role), getOrg(orgId), updateOrg(orgId, data)

tasks.title: Admin Panel â€” LLM API Key Management
tasks.description: Secure storage of per-org LLM provider API keys
steps: Create supabase/migrations/011_llm_keys.sql: llm_provider_keys table (id, org_id FK, provider, encrypted_key, created_by FK, created_at, updated_at)
Create src/lib/crypto.ts: encryptApiKey(key) and decryptApiKey(encryptedKey) using AES-256-GCM with ENCRYPTION_KEY env var

Create src/lib/db/llm-keys.ts: setProviderKey(orgId, provider, key), getProviderKey(orgId, provider) â€” returns decrypted key (server-side only), listProviderKeys(orgId) â€” returns providers list WITHOUT keys
Create src/app/api/admin/keys/route.ts: GET (list configured providers, no key values exposed)
Create src/app/api/admin/keys/[provider]/route.ts: PUT { apiKey } to set/update key, DELETE to remove key

Create src/app/(app)/admin/keys/page.tsx: LLM API Keys page. Show table of providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, Gemini) with configured/not-configured status. Edit button opens modal to enter new key (masked input). Keys are never shown after saving
Update runtime executor to use org keys from DB (decrypt at execution time)

tasks.title: Admin Panel â€” Audit Logs
tasks.description: View security audit log of all user actions
steps: Create src/lib/db/audit.ts: logAction(userId, orgId, action, resourceType, resourceId, metadata, ipAddress) â€” insert into audit_logs table. writeLog() should be fire-and-forget (non-blocking)
Create src/app/api/admin/audit/route.ts: GET with pagination (page, limit), filter by user, action, resource_type, date range
Create src/app/(app)/admin/audit/page.tsx: audit log viewer. Table with columns: timestamp, user, action, resource type, resource ID, IP address
Add filter controls: user search, action type filter, date range picker
Add CSV export button
Integrate logAction() calls in: user role changes, solution publish/delete, KB create/delete, run execution, API key changes
Create src/components/admin/AuditTable.tsx: paginated audit log table component

tasks.title: Error Handling & Loading States
tasks.description: Global error boundaries, loading skeletons, and toast notifications
steps: Create src/app/error.tsx: global error boundary page with error message and 'Try again' button
Create src/app/not-found.tsx: 404 page
Create src/app/(app)/solutions/[id]/error.tsx: solution-specific error boundary
Create src/lib/errors.ts: AppError class, error code constants, formatError(e) utility
Create src/components/ui/ErrorAlert.tsx: inline error alert component
Create src/hooks/useToast.ts: hook for showing toast notifications (success, error, info, warning)
Add skeleton loading states to: SolutionCard, KBCard, MarketplaceCard, DocumentList, RunHistoryList
All API routes return consistent error format: { error: { code, message } }
Show toast on: solution save, KB creation, document upload complete, run complete/error, role change

tasks.title: Responsive Design & Mobile Adaptation
tasks.description: Ensure the platform works on tablet and desktop screen sizes
steps: Sidebar: collapse to icon-only rail on md screens, hide completely on sm screens with hamburger toggle
Create src/components/layout/MobileNav.tsx: bottom nav bar for mobile with main section icons
Solution cards grid: 1 col on mobile, 2 col on md, 3 col on lg
Builder canvas: add warning banner on mobile ('Best experienced on desktop'). Canvas still functional but limited screen space
Knowledge base document upload: works on mobile via tap-to-select fallback
Marketplace grid: 1 col mobile, 2 col tablet, 3 col desktop
Admin tables: horizontal scroll on mobile
Test all pages at 375px (mobile), 768px (tablet), 1280px (desktop)

tasks.title: Final Integration Testing & Build Verification
tasks.description: End-to-end testing of core flows and production build
steps: Run npm run lint â€” fix all errors
Run npm run build â€” ensure production build succeeds with no TypeScript errors
Browser test Flow 1 (Auth): navigate to /login, click Azure AD sign-in, complete SSO, verify dashboard loads
Browser test Flow 2 (Build): create new solution, choose Sequential architecture, configure 2 agent nodes with different LLM providers and system prompts, save workflow
Browser test Flow 3 (Knowledge Base): create KB, upload a PDF, wait for status to change to 'ready', run a semantic search and verify results
Browser test Flow 4 (Run): run a configured solution with a sample security risk question, verify streaming output appears
Browser test Flow 5 (Marketplace): publish a solution, browse marketplace, find it, copy to My Solutions
Browser test Flow 6 (Admin): as admin, change a user's role, set an LLM API key, view audit log
Verify all actions appear in audit log
Verify RLS â€” user cannot access another org's solutions via direct API call
